(born 13 June 1998) is an English [professional](/wiki/Professional_sports "Professional sports") [rugby union](/wiki/Rugby_union "Rugby union") player for [Hartpury University](/wiki/Hartpury_University_R.F.C. "Hartpury University R.F.C.") in the [Champ Rugby](/wiki/Champ_Rugby "Champ Rugby").He plays as a centre or fly-half.[[1]](#cite_note-1) He previously played for [Harlequins](/wiki/Harlequin_F.C. "Harlequin F.C."),[[2]](#cite_note-2) [London Scottish](/wiki/London_Scottish_F.C. "London Scottish F.C.")[[3]](#cite_note-3) in the [RFU Championship](/wiki/RFU_Championship "RFU Championship"),[[4]](#cite_note-4) [Luctonians](/wiki/Luctonians "Luctonians") and [Birmingham Moseley](/wiki/Birmingham_Moseley_Rugby "Birmingham Moseley Rugby")[[5]](#cite_note-5)
["Scots add two young exciting backs from National One"](https://londonscottish.com/scots-add-two-young-exciting-backs-from-national-one/). 3 April 2019.
["London Scottish all set for derby encounter"](https://londonscottish.com/london-scottish-all-set-for-derby-encounter/). 24 January 2020.
["Worcester Warriors v Newcastle Falcons - Singha Premiership Rugby 7s"](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=G37sCFHdcFo). *[YouTube](/wiki/YouTube "YouTube")*. 30 July 2017.

["Worcester Warriors U18s thrash Exeter Chiefs on Academy Finals Day"](https://www.worcesternews.co.uk/sport/14278386.worcester-warriors-u18s-thrash-exeter-chiefs-on-academy-finals-day/). 15 February 2016.
["Lucs left frustrated by second half fightback"](https://www.herefordtimes.com/sport/18015163.luctonians-left-frustrated-second-half-fightback/). *Hereford Times*. 5 November 2019.

["RUGBY: Smith delighted that Luctonians have secured their National league 2 North status"](https://www.herefordtimes.com/sport/15219369.luctonians-captain-alex-smith-on-side-securing-national-league-two-north-status/). *Hereford Times*. 12 April 2017.
["Live Stream: Modus Cup 2022 | RGS Worcester v King's Worcester – NextGenXV"](https://nextgenxv.com/2022/11/04/live-stream-modus-cup-2022-rgs-worcester-v-kings-worcester/).

**Oliver "Ollie" Allsopp** (born 13 June 1998) is an English [professional](/wiki/Professional_sports "Professional sports") [rugby union](/wiki/Rugby_union "Rugby union") player for [Hartpury University](/wiki/Hartpury_University_R.F.C. "Hartpury University R.F.C.") in the [Champ Rugby](/wiki/Champ_Rugby "Champ Rugby").He plays as a centre or fly-half.[[1]](#cite_note-1) He previously played for [Harlequins](/wiki/Harlequin_F.C. "Harlequin F.C."),[[2]](#cite_note-2) [London Scottish](/wiki/London_Scottish_F.C. "London Scottish F.C.")[[3]](#cite_note-3) in the [RFU Championship](/wiki/RFU_Championship "RFU Championship"),[[4]](#cite_note-4) [Luctonians](/wiki/Luctonians "Luctonians") and [Birmingham Moseley](/wiki/Birmingham_Moseley_Rugby "Birmingham Moseley Rugby")[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ollie_Allsopp&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Ollie attended [Malvern College](/wiki/Malvern_College "Malvern College") [[6]](#cite_note-6) where he was 1XV rugby captain, and played there with [James Scott (rugby union)](/wiki/James_Scott_(rugby_union) "James Scott (rugby union)"). Whilst at Malvern, Ollie was also 1XI cricket and played alongside [Jack Haynes](/wiki/Jack_Haynes "Jack Haynes") and [Zen Malik](/wiki/Zen_Malik "Zen Malik") with their head of cricket [Mark Hardinges](/wiki/Mark_Hardinges "Mark Hardinges").

During Allsopp's youth career, he first played rugby at [Chaddesley Corbett](/wiki/Chaddesley_Corbett "Chaddesley Corbett") rugby club near Kidderminster before transferring to Worcester RFC before slowly progressing into [Worcester Warriors](/wiki/Worcester_Warriors "Worcester Warriors") academy and playing U18s level.[[7]](#cite_note-7) He also represented Worcester in the [2017 Premiership Rugby Sevens Series](/wiki/2017_Premiership_Rugby_Sevens_Series "2017 Premiership Rugby Sevens Series") at [Franklin's Gardens](/wiki/Franklin%27s_Gardens "Franklin's Gardens") and scored in their 24-28 loss against [Newcastle Falcons](/wiki/Newcastle_Falcons "Newcastle Falcons") in the Cup quarter-final.[[8]](#cite_note-8). Ollie also had numerous appearances for Worcester at [Premiership Rugby Shield](/wiki/Premiership_Rugby_Shield "Premiership Rugby Shield") level for them, alongside fellow professionals [Ted Hill](/wiki/Ted_Hill_(rugby_union) "Ted Hill (rugby union)"), [Jamie Shillcock](/wiki/Jamie_Shillcock "Jamie Shillcock") and [Will Butler](/wiki/Will_Butler_(rugby_union) "Will Butler (rugby union)").[[9]](#cite_note-9)

Allsopp then went to study at [Hartpury University](/wiki/Hartpury_University "Hartpury University") where he was also involved in the team that won the [BUCS Super Rugby](/wiki/BUCS_Super_Rugby "BUCS Super Rugby") championship in 2016-17 against [University of Exeter](/wiki/University_of_Exeter "University of Exeter") at [Twickenham Stadium](/wiki/Twickenham_Stadium "Twickenham Stadium") in a team captained by [Sebastian Negri](/wiki/Sebastian_Negri "Sebastian Negri") and consisting of [Harry Randall](/wiki/Harry_Randall_(rugby_union) "Harry Randall (rugby union)") and [Alex Craig](/wiki/Alex_Craig_(rugby_union) "Alex Craig (rugby union)").

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ollie_Allsopp&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

While still a student at [Hartpury University](/wiki/Hartpury_University "Hartpury University"), Ollie signed for [Luctonians](/wiki/Luctonians "Luctonians") where he was also a crucial part of their team[[10]](#cite_note-10) and survival in [National League 2 North](/wiki/National_League_2_North "National League 2 North") in the 2017-18 season.[[11]](#cite_note-11).

He made his Moseley debut at home against [Darlington Mowden Park](/wiki/Darlington_Mowden_Park_R.F.C. "Darlington Mowden Park R.F.C.") and was also involved in the clubs historic pre season 24-15 win against [Worcester Warriors](/wiki/Worcester_Warriors "Worcester Warriors") in 2017.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

On the 3rd of April 2019, Allsopp signed for [London Scottish F.C.](/wiki/London_Scottish_F.C. "London Scottish F.C."),[[13]](#cite_note-13) where he gained 4 appearances for the London side. He made his debut in a pre-season encounter against [Edinburgh Rugby](/wiki/Edinburgh_Rugby "Edinburgh Rugby") at [Murrayfield Stadium](/wiki/Murrayfield_Stadium "Murrayfield Stadium").[[14]](#cite_note-14) While at the club, Allsopp went on loan to [Luctonians](/wiki/Luctonians "Luctonians") and [Harlequins F.C.](/wiki/Harlequins_F.C. "Harlequins F.C.") in the [Premiership Rugby Shield](/wiki/Premiership_Rugby_Shield "Premiership Rugby Shield").

Allsopp then returned back to [Birmingham Moseley Rugby](/wiki/Birmingham_Moseley_Rugby "Birmingham Moseley Rugby"). He also worked at [Royal Grammar School Worcester](/wiki/Royal_Grammar_School_Worcester "Royal Grammar School Worcester")[[15]](#cite_note-15) during his first tenure at Moseley as a sport assistant alongside head of rugby [Robert Lewis](/wiki/Robert_Lewis_(rugby_union) "Robert Lewis (rugby union)") and [Aleki Lutui](/wiki/Aleki_Lutui "Aleki Lutui"). In 2022, Allsopp coached RGS to a [Modus Cup](/wiki/Modus_Cup "Modus Cup") victory 20-17 alongside his other coaches [Robert Lewis](/wiki/Robert_Lewis_(rugby_union) "Robert Lewis (rugby union)") and [Aleki Lutui](/wiki/Aleki_Lutui "Aleki Lutui").[[16]](#cite_note-16). Allsopp re-joined [RGS Worcester](/wiki/RGS_Worcester "RGS Worcester") coaching team for the 2025 edition of the [Modus Cup](/wiki/Modus_Cup "Modus Cup") and coached them to a 21-20 victory.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

Ollie proved critical for [Birmingham Moseley Rugby](/wiki/Birmingham_Moseley_Rugby "Birmingham Moseley Rugby")[[18]](#cite_note-18) winning their player of the season in the 2023/24 season[[19]](#cite_note-19) in a pivotal season for the club where they finished third place in the [National League One](/wiki/National_League_One "National League One") table in a team consisting of [Greg Fisilau](/wiki/Greg_Fisilau "Greg Fisilau"), [Ashley Johnson](/wiki/Ashley_Johnson_(rugby_union) "Ashley Johnson (rugby union)"), [Tuoyo Egodo](/wiki/Tuoyo_Egodo "Tuoyo Egodo"), [Seb Nagle-Taylor](/wiki/Seb_Nagle-Taylor "Seb Nagle-Taylor") and [Rekeiti Ma'asi-White](/wiki/Rekeiti_Ma%27asi-White "Rekeiti Ma'asi-White"). On 13 May 2025, it was announced Allsopp would be leaving Birmingham Moseley.[[20]](#cite_note-20)
On 4 September 2025, it was announced Allsopp would sign for [Hartpury University](/wiki/Hartpury_University_R.F.C. "Hartpury University R.F.C.") in [Champ Rugby](/wiki/Champ_Rugby "Champ Rugby").

Allsopp made his non competitive debut starting at 12 against [Dragons](/wiki/Dragons_RFC "Dragons RFC") in a pre season encounter that Hartpury lost 45-12.[[21]](#cite_note-21) and made his competitive debut for Hartpury against [Cornish Pirates](/wiki/Cornish_Pirates "Cornish Pirates") on the opening round of the 2025/26 Champ Rugby season on the 4 October 2025 [[22]](#cite_note-22). Allsopp was also involved in their historic 28-25 win over [Worcester Warriors](/wiki/Worcester_Warriors "Worcester Warriors")[[23]](#cite_note-23) which was the first ever encounter between the two clubs. Additionally, Allsopp put in a man of the match performance on the 25 October, where he played against [Chinnor R.F.C.](/wiki/Chinnor_R.F.C. "Chinnor R.F.C."), which was also the game where he scored his first try for Hartpury.[[24]](#cite_note-24) Allsopp then put in a team of the week performance in round 13 against [Worcester Warriors](/wiki/Worcester_Warriors "Worcester Warriors") at [Sixways Stadium](/wiki/Sixways_Stadium "Sixways Stadium") in their historic 34-28 win over the team.[[25]](#cite_note-25)
